title: Black Panther Party 10-Point Platform and Program (German translation)
description: German translation of the Black Panther Party's founding 10-point platform, scanned from the German anthology Black Power (ID-Verlag) and held in the Rote Armee Fraktion solidarity collection.

title: White Mother Country Radicals (German translation, Bernardine Dohrn)
description: German translation of Bernardine Dohrn's foundational 1968 Weather Underground essay calling on white radicals to organize in support of the Black liberation struggle. From the RAF Mappe 18: USA solidarity folder.

title: New Morning Changing Weather (German translation, Bernardine Dohrn)
description: German translation of Bernardine Dohrn's December 1970 Weather Underground communique. From the RAF Mappe 18: USA solidarity folder.

title: Fascism (George Jackson)
description: Book chapter on fascism by Soledad Brother George Jackson, killed at San Quentin in August 1971. From the RAF Mappe 18: USA solidarity folder.

title: George Jackson 1941–1971 (Memorial Poster)
description: Marcus Books San Francisco memorial poster for George Jackson, killed at San Quentin August 1971. Scanned from the RAF Mappe 18: USA solidarity folder.

title: Geschichte ist eine Waffe — Flyer on the Murder of George Jackson (1986)
description: German AgipA Press flyer commemorating the 15th anniversary of the August 21, 1971 killing of George Jackson at San Quentin.

title: Memorial Service for George Jackson (The Black Panther, Sept 4 1971)
description: Twenty-page issue of The Black Panther newspaper covering George Jackson's memorial service, scanned from the RAF Mappe 18: USA folder.

title: Prairie Fire (Weather Underground)
description: Full 160-page text of the Weather Underground Organization's 1974 political manifesto Prairie Fire. From the RAF Mappe 18: USA folder.

title: Osawatomie (Weather Underground Organization Journal, 1976)
description: April/May 1976 issue of the Weather Underground Organization's journal Osawatomie, scanned from the RAF Mappe 18: USA folder.

title: Political Statement (George Jackson Brigade)
description: 1977 political statement of the George Jackson Brigade, the Pacific Northwest armed clandestine group whose members (Ed Mead, Bo Brown, Rita Brown, John Sherman) became long-term US political prisoners.

title: George Jackson Brigade — Communique on the Mercedes-Benz Attack (English)
description: English-language communique on the November 1977 George Jackson Brigade attack on a Mercedes-Benz dealership, scanned from the RAF Mappe 18: USA folder.

title: George Jackson Brigade — Communique on the Mercedes-Benz Attack (German)
description: German translation of the George Jackson Brigade's November 1977 Mercedes-Benz attack communique.

title: The Complete Communiques (George Jackson Brigade, 1975–1977)
description: Complete collected communiques of the George Jackson Brigade, the Pacific Northwest armed clandestine group active 1975–1977.

title: On the Hunger Strike of Emily Harris (German)
description: German solidarity flyer for the November 1977 hunger strike of Symbionese Liberation Army member Emily Harris while in US custody.

title: Statements by Political Prisoners in the USA (German, 1985)
description: German-language statements by U.S. political prisoners including Resistance Conspiracy Case defendant Tim Blunk, 1985. From the RAF Mappe 18: USA folder.

title: Statements by Political Prisoners in the USA (Libertad magazine, 1985)
description: Twenty-seven-page collection of statements by U.S. political prisoners — including FALN/Puerto Rican independentista Edwin Cortes — published in Libertad magazine, 1985. From the RAF Mappe 18: USA folder.

title: Communiques from the Armed Clandestine Movement (1985)
description: Sixty-two-page anthology of communiques from U.S. armed-left organizations, 1985. From the RAF Mappe 18: USA folder.

title: Call Against the Death Sentence of Mumia Abu-Jamal (German, 1989)
description: German AgipA Press solidarity appeal against the death sentence of Mumia Abu-Jamal, October 1989.

title: Kommando George Jackson — Rhein-Main Air Base Attack Communique (1985)
description: Trilingual (German/English/French) communique on the August 8, 1985 RAF action against the U.S. Rhein-Main Air Base, named for George Jackson — illustrating the transnational naming of US political prisoners by European armed groups.

title: Sacco and Vanzetti Memorial Print (Le Reveil anarchiste, 1928)
description: Memorial print by Swiss engraver Alexandre Mairet, published in the Genevan anarchist paper Le Reveil anarchiste no. 743 on May 1, 1928 — eight months after the executions. Held by the Centre International de Recherches sur l'Anarchisme (CIRA), Switzerland.
